name: Ala Moana Beach Park (Magic Island Reef)
tagline: An urban reef snorkel that Honolulu locals hit on their lunch break.
parking: Large free lot at Ala Moana Beach Park; metered spots along the peninsula
hours: Park open 4am–10pm; gates lock at night
bestTime: Morning before trade winds chop up the surface; summer months for clearest water
talkStory: The reef off Magic Island's channel wall is home to endemic Hawaiian whitespotted toby and fourspot butterflyfish you won't see on the mainland. This area was dredged coral fill when the peninsula was built in 1964, and the reef has been slowly reclaiming it ever since — a quiet conservation success story. Experienced locals snorkel the outer channel edge at slack tide for the best visibility and biggest honu sightings; avoid afternoons when trade-wind chop turns it milky.
